whichDataset.description: Heading for the question about which dataset the user is reviewing.

useDoiUrl.message: Use the dataset DOI or URL.
useDoiUrl.description: Instruction alongside the question “Which dataset are you reviewing?”.

whatIsDoi.description: Heading for the explanation of what a DOI is.

whatIsDoiText.message: A <doi>DOI</doi> is a unique identifier that you can find on many datasets. For example, {example} or {exampleUrl}.
whatIsDoiText.description: Text following the heading “What is a DOI?” that explains what a DOI is and gives examples of what a DOI looks like.

errorEnterADataset.message: Enter a dataset DOI or URL
errorEnterADataset.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Which dataset are you reviewing?” with an invalid value.

errorEnterTheDataset.message: Enter the dataset DOI or URL
errorEnterTheDataset.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Which dataset are you reviewing?” without entering a value.

dontKnowDataset.message: Sorry, we don’t know this dataset
dontKnowDataset.description: Title of the page following the form “Which dataset are you reviewing?” when we don’t recognize the dataset DOI or URL.

doiCouldBeDryad.message: We think the DOI {doi} could be a Dryad dataset, but we can’t find any details.
doiCouldBeDryad.description: Message shown alongside the title “Sorry, we don’t know this dataset”.

doiCouldBeScielo.message: We think the DOI {doi} could be a SciELO dataset, but we can’t find any details.
doiCouldBeScielo.description: Message shown alongside the title “Sorry, we don’t know this dataset”.

checkCorrectDoi.message: If you typed the DOI, check it is correct.
checkCorrectDoi.description: Message shown alongside the title “Sorry, we don’t know this dataset”.

checkPastedDoi.message: If you pasted the DOI, check you copied the entire address.
checkPastedDoi.description: Message shown alongside the title “Sorry, we don’t know this dataset”.

doiIsCorrect.message: If the DOI is correct, please <contact>get in touch</contact>.
doiIsCorrect.description: Message shown alongside the title “Sorry, we don’t know this dataset”.

unsupportedDoi.message: Sorry, we don’t support this DOI
unsupportedDoi.description: Title of the page following the form “Which dataset are you reviewing?” when we recognize the DOI format but don’t support the repository it’s from.

unsupportedUrl.message: Sorry, we don’t support this URL
unsupportedUrl.description: Title of the page following the form “Which dataset are you reviewing?” when we can’t workout the dataset DOI from the URL.

supportDatasetsFrom.message: We support datasets from Dryad and SciELO.
supportDatasetsFrom.description: Message shown alongside the titles “Sorry, we don’t support this DOI”, “Sorry, we don’t support this URL” and “Sorry, we only support datasets”.

doiIsForDataset.message: If this DOI is for a dataset on a repository we don’t support, please <contact>get in touch</contact>.
doiIsForDataset.description: Message shown alongside the title “Sorry, we don’t support this DOI”.

urlIsForDataset.message: If this URL is for a dataset on a repository we don’t support, please <contact>get in touch</contact>.
urlIsForDataset.description: Message shown alongside the title “Sorry, we don’t support this URL”.

tryDoi.message: Otherwise, if the dataset has a DOI, please try using that instead.
tryDoi.description: Message shown alongside the titles “Sorry, we don’t support this URL”.

notADataset.description: Title of the page following the form “Which dataset are you reviewing?” when the DOI or URL entered is not for a dataset.

isADataset.message: If this is a dataset, please <contact>get in touch</contact>.
isADataset.description: Message shown alongside the title “Sorry, we only support datasets”.

reviewADataset.description: Title of the page where the user can continue the process of reviewing a dataset.

backToDataset.description: Link that takes the user back to the dataset page.

authoredBy.description: Byline that shows the authors of the dataset being reviewed.

youCanWriteAPrereview.message: You can write a PREreview of {dataset}. We’ll ask questions about the dataset to create a structured review.
youCanWriteAPrereview.description: Message shown alongside the title “Review a dataset” when the user hasn’t already started a PREreview of the dataset.

asYouHaveAlreadyStarted.message: As you’ve already started a PREreview of {dataset}, we’ll take you to the next step so you can carry on.
asYouHaveAlreadyStarted.description: Message shown alongside the title “Review a dataset” when the user has already started a PREreview of the dataset.

beforeStart.description: Heading for the section that contains information about what the user needs to know before starting a PREreview of the dataset.

orcidIdLogIn.message: We will ask you to log in with your ORCID iD. If you don’t have an iD, you can create one.
orcidIdLogIn.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Before you start”.

whatIsOrcidIdHeading.description: Heading for the explanation of what an ORCID iD is.

whatIsOrcidId.message: An <link>ORCID iD</link> is a unique identifier that distinguishes you from everyone with the same or similar name.
whatIsOrcidId.description: Text following the heading “What is an ORCID iD?” explaining what an ORCID iD is.

checkYourPrereview.description: Title of the page where the user can check their PREreview before publishing it.

datasetDetails.description: Heading for the section that contains details about the dataset being reviewed.

title.description: Label for the title of the dataset being reviewed.

posted.description: Label for the date that the dataset being reviewed was posted.

repository.description: Label for the repository containing the dataset being reviewed.

yourDetails.description: Heading for the section that contains the user’s details.

publishedName.description: Label for the published name the user has chosen.

changePublishedName.description: Link that allows the user to change their published name.

competingInterests.description: Label for the section that contains information about the user’s competing interests.

noneDeclared.description: Text indicating that the user has declared no competing interests.

changeCompetingInterests.description: Link that allows the user to change their competing interests.

yourReview.description: Heading for the section the shows the user’s review of the dataset.

rateQuality.message: How would you rate the quality of this data set?
rateQuality.description: Label for the question about rating the quality of the dataset.

selectRateQuality.message: Select how you rate the quality
selectRateQuality.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?” without selecting a value.

excellent.description: Option for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”

excellentTip.message: There are few, if any, noticeable errors, inconsistencies, missing values, or other issues in this dataset.
excellentTip.description: Tip shown alongside the option “Excellent” for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”

excellentWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Excellent” rating for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”.

fair.description: Option for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”

fairTip.message: There are some noticeable errors, inconsistencies, missing values, or other issues in this dataset, but it is still intact enough to trust.
fairTip.description: Tip shown alongside the option “Fair” for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”

fairWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Fair” rating for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”.

poor.description: Option for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”

poorTip.message: There are many noticeable errors, inconsistencies, missing values, or other issues in the dataset that make it too difficult to trust.
poorTip.description: Tip shown alongside the option “Poor” for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”

poorWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Poor” rating for the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”.

dontKnow.description: Option for indicating that the user can’t answer the question.

changeQualityRating.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “How would you rate the quality of this dataset?”.

followFairAndCare.message: Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?
followFairAndCare.description: Label for the question about whether the dataset follows FAIR and CARE principles.

selectFollowFairAndCare.message: Select if the dataset follows FAIR and CARE principles
selectFollowFairAndCare.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?” without selecting an option.

followFairAndCareYesTip.message: The dataset has enough metadata and follows the FAIR and CARE principles.
followFairAndCareYesTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Yes” option for the question “Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?”.

followFairAndCareYesWhy.message: How does it follow the principles?
followFairAndCareYesWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Yes” answer to the question “Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?”.

followFairAndCarePartlyTip.message: The dataset has some metadata and follows some FAIR and CARE principles, but minor problems exist.
followFairAndCarePartlyTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Partly” option for the question “Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?”.

followFairAndCarePartlyWhy.message: How does it partly follow the principles?
followFairAndCarePartlyWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Partly” answer to the question “Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?”.

followFairAndCareNoTip.message: The dataset lacks enough metadata and does not follow the FAIR and CARE principles.
followFairAndCareNoTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “No” option for the question “Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?”.

followFairAndCareNoWhy.message: How does it not follow the principles?
followFairAndCareNoWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “No” answer to the question “Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?”.

yes.description: Option for indicating that the user agrees with the statement.

no.description: Option for indicating that the user disagrees with the statement.

partly.description: Option for indicating that the user partially agrees with the statement.

changeFairAndCare.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset follows FAIR and CARE principles</visuallyHidden>
changeFairAndCare.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Does this dataset follow FAIR and CARE principles?”.

enoughMetadata.message: Does the dataset have enough metadata?
enoughMetadata.description: Label for the question about whether the dataset has enough metadata.

selectEnoughMetadata.message: Select if the dataset has enough metadata
selectEnoughMetadata.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Does the dataset have enough metadata?” without selecting an option.

enoughMetadataYesTip.message: The dataset includes enough metadata such as its datatype, tools and methods used to gather and analyze the data, ethical considerations used during data collection and analysis, and support documentation such as a glossary, README, or software recommendations.
enoughMetadataYesTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Yes” option for the question “Does the dataset have enough metadata?”.

enoughMetadataYesWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Yes” answer to the question “Does the dataset have enough metadata?”.

enoughMetadataPartlyTip.message: The dataset includes some of the information listed above, but more is needed to fully understand how the data were gathered, analyzed, and otherwise used.
enoughMetadataPartlyTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Partly” option for the question “Does the dataset have enough metadata?”.

enoughMetadataPartlyWhy.message: What metadata does it have, and what is missing?
enoughMetadataPartlyWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Partly” answer to the question “Does the dataset have enough metadata?”.

enoughMetadataNoTip.message: The dataset does not include enough metadata to help understand how the data were gathered, analyzed, and otherwise used.
enoughMetadataNoTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “No” option for the question “Does the dataset have enough metadata?”.

enoughMetadataNoWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “No” answer to the question “Does the dataset have enough metadata?”.

changeEnoughMetadata.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset has enough metadata</visuallyHidden>
changeEnoughMetadata.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Does the dataset have enough metadata?”.

trackChanges.message: Does this dataset include a way to list or track changes or versions? If so, does it seem accurate?
trackChanges.description: Label for the question about whether the dataset tracks changes.

selectTrackChanges.message: Select if the dataset has a way to list or track changes or versions
selectTrackChanges.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Does this dataset include a way to list or track changes or versions? If so, does it seem accurate?” without selecting an option.

trackChangesYesTip.message: The dataset has a clear version history that appears to be accurate and up-to-date.
trackChangesYesTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Yes” option for the question “Does this dataset include a way to list or track changes or versions? If so, does it seem accurate?”.

trackChangesYesWhy.message: How does the dataset share its metadata?
trackChangesYesWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Yes” answer to the question “Does this dataset include a way to list or track changes or versions? If so, does it seem accurate?”.

trackChangesPartlyTip.message: The dataset includes a way to list or track changes or versions, but it doesn’t seem to be accurate or current.
trackChangesPartlyTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Partly” option for the question “Does this dataset include a way to list or track changes or versions? If so, does it seem accurate?”.

trackChangesPartlyWhy.message: How does the dataset share its metadata? What’s missing?
trackChangesPartlyWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Partly” answer to the question “Does this dataset include a way to list or track changes or versions? If so, does it seem accurate?”.

trackChangesNoWhy.message: How should the dataset present its missing metadata?
trackChangesNoWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “No” answer to the question “Does this dataset include a way to list or track changes or versions? If so, does it seem accurate?”.

changeTrackChanges.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset includes a way to list or track changes or versions</visuallyHidden>
changeTrackChanges.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Does this dataset include a way to list or track changes or versions?”.

signsOfAlteration.message: Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?
signsOfAlteration.description: Label for the question about whether data has been censored or deleted.

selectSignsOfAlteration.message: Select if the dataset shows signs of alteration
selectSignsOfAlteration.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?” without selecting an option.

signsOfAlterationYesTip.message: The dataset shows clear signs of alteration that are not accounted for by records of its versioning; some kind of censorship, deletion, or redaction is evident throughout.
signsOfAlterationYesTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Yes” option for the question “Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?”.

signsOfAlterationYesWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Yes” answer to the question “Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?”.

signsOfAlterationPartlyTip.message: Some parts of this dataset show signs of alterations that are not accounted for by records of its versioning; some kind of censorship, deletion, or redaction is evident throughout.
signsOfAlterationPartlyTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Partly” option for the question “Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?”.

signsOfAlterationPartlyWhy.message: Which parts seem intact? Which parts seem altered? How do they seem altered?
signsOfAlterationPartlyWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Partly” answer to the question “Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?”.

signsOfAlterationNoTip.message: The dataset does not show obvious signs of alteration.
signsOfAlterationNoTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “No” option for the question “Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?”.

signsOfAlterationNoWhy.message: What makes this dataset seem mostly intact to you? What trust markers make you believe it hasn’t been altered?
signsOfAlterationNoWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “No” answer to the question “Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?”.

changeSignsOfAlteration.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset shows signs of alteration</visuallyHidden>
changeSignsOfAlteration.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Does this dataset show signs of alteration beyond instances of likely human error, such as censorship, deletion, or redaction, that are not accounted for otherwise?”.

suitedForPurpose.message: Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?
suitedForPurpose.description: Label for the question about whether the dataset is appropriate for this kind of research.

selectSuitedForPurpose.message: Select if the dataset is well-suited
selectSuitedForPurpose.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?” without selecting an option.

suitedForPurposeYesTip.message: The way these data were collected, analyzed, and used is well-suited to support its stated research purpose.
suitedForPurposeYesTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Yes” option for the question “Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?”.

suitedForPurposeYesWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Yes” answer to the question “Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?”.

suitedForPurposePartlyTip.message: The way these data were collected, analyzed, and used partly supports its stated research purpose, but other questions, methods, or tools would have been better.
suitedForPurposePartlyTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Partly” option for the question “Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?”.

suitedForPurposePartlyWhy.message: How is it only partly well-suited? Which parts seem well-suited? Which parts don’t?
suitedForPurposePartlyWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Partly” answer to the question “Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?”.

suitedForPurposeNoTip.message: The way these data were collected, analyzed, and used is not well-suited for its stated research purpose.
suitedForPurposeNoTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “No” option for the question “Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?”.

suitedForPurposeNoWhy.message: How is it not well-suited for its purpose?
suitedForPurposeNoWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “No” answer to the question “Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?”.

changeSuitedForPurpose.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Is the dataset well-suited to support its stated research purpose?”.

supportsConclusion.message: Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?
supportsConclusion.description: Label for the question about whether the dataset supports supports related conclusions.

selectSupportsConclusion.message: Select if the dataset supports the conclusions
selectSupportsConclusion.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?” without selecting an option.

supportsConclusionYesTip.message: This dataset clearly supports the researcher’s conclusions about it—or is likely to support clear conclusions—and does not require creative interpretation or overreach to do so.
supportsConclusionYesTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Yes” option for the question “Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?”.

supportsConclusionYesWhy.message: How does it support the conclusions?
supportsConclusionYesWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Yes” answer to the question “Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?”.

supportsConclusionPartlyTip.message: This dataset supports some of the researcher’s conclusions about it—or is likely to support some clear conclusions—but does not clearly support some of the researcher’s conclusions or is unlikely to do so because of minor issues.
supportsConclusionPartlyTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Partly” option for the question “Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?”.

supportsConclusionPartlyWhy.message: How does it partly support the conclusions?
supportsConclusionPartlyWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Partly” answer to the question “Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?”.

supportsConclusionNoTip.message: This dataset does not support the researcher’s conclusions about it—or it is unlikely to support any clear conclusions—without flawed analysis or overreach.
supportsConclusionNoTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “No” option for the question “Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?”.

supportsConclusionNoWhy.message: How does it not support the conclusions?
supportsConclusionNoWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “No” answer to the question “Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?”.

changeSupportsConclusion.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset supports the conclusions</visuallyHidden>
changeSupportsConclusion.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Does this dataset support the researcher’s stated conclusions?”.

granularEnough.message: Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?
granularEnough.description: Label for the question about whether the data is detailed enough.

selectGranularEnough.message: Select if the dataset is granular enough
selectGranularEnough.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?” without selecting an option.

granularEnoughYesTip.message: The dataset provides data at a detailed or granular enough level to seem trustworthy as a standard of measurement or truth.
granularEnoughYesTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Yes” option for the question “Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?”.

granularEnoughYesWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Yes” answer to the question “Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?”.

granularEnoughPartlyTip.message: Some of the data in the dataset provides data at a detailed or granular enough level to seem trustworthy as a standard of measurement or truth, but not all of it.
granularEnoughPartlyTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Partly” option for the question “Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?”.

granularEnoughPartlyWhy.message: How is it only partly granular enough? Which parts need to be more detailed?
granularEnoughPartlyWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Partly” answer to the question “Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?”.

granularEnoughNoTip.message: The dataset doesn’t provide data at a detailed or granular enough level to seem trustworthy as a standard of measurement or truth.
granularEnoughNoTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “No” option for the question “Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?”.

granularEnoughNoWhy.message: How is it not detailed enough?
granularEnoughNoWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “No” answer to the question “Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?”.

changeGranularEnough.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset is granular enough</visuallyHidden>
changeGranularEnough.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Is the dataset granular enough to be a reliable standard of measurement?”.

relativelyErrorFree.description: Label for the question about whether the dataset is error-free.

relativelyErrorFreeTip.message: Errors might include things like using inappropriate data collection or analysis methods, using inappropriate metrics with the data, or manipulating the data in other ways that result in errors in the dataset.
relativelyErrorFreeTip.description: Tip shown alongside the question “Is the dataset relatively error-free?”.

selectRelativelyErrorFree.message: Select if the dataset is relatively error-free
selectRelativelyErrorFree.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Is the dataset relatively error-free?” without selecting an option.

relativelyErrorFreeYesTip.message: Few, if any errors, are in the dataset, and any errors are minor.
relativelyErrorFreeYesTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Yes” option for the question “Is the dataset relatively error-free?”.

relativelyErrorFreeYesWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Yes” answer to the question “Is the dataset relatively error-free?”.

relativelyErrorFreePartlyTip.message: Several minor errors or even one major error are in the dataset.
relativelyErrorFreePartlyTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “Partly” option for the question “Is the dataset relatively error-free?”.

relativelyErrorFreePartlyWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Partly” answer to the question “Is the dataset relatively error-free?”.

relativelyErrorFreeNoTip.message: Many minor and major errors are in the dataset.
relativelyErrorFreeNoTip.description: Tip shown alongside the “No” option for the question “Is the dataset relatively error-free?”.

relativelyErrorFreeNoWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “No” answer to the question “Is the dataset relatively error-free?”.

changeRelativelyErrorFree.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset is relatively error-free</visuallyHidden>
changeRelativelyErrorFree.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question about whether the dataset is error-free.

howConsequential.message: Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?
howConsequential.description: Label for the question about the dataset mattering to its audience.

selectHowConsequential.message: Select how consequential is it likely to seem
selectHowConsequential.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the form “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?” without selecting an option.

veryConsequential.description: Option for the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

veryConsequentialTip.message: The dataset is likely to be very consequential for a broad audience.
veryConsequentialTip.description: Tip shown alongside the option “Very consequential” for the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

veryConsequentialWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Very consequential” answer to the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

somewhatConsequential.description: Option for the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

somewhatConsequentialTip.message: Several minor errors or even one major error are in the dataset.
somewhatConsequentialTip.description: Tip shown alongside the option “Somewhat consequential” for the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

somewhatConsequentialWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Somewhat consequential” answer to the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

notConsequential.description: Option for the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

notConsequentialTip.message: The dataset isn’t likely to be consequential for its intended audience.
notConsequentialTip.description: Tip shown alongside the option “Not consequential” for the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

notConsequentialWhy.description: Label for the text field to provide context for the “Not consequential” answer to the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

changeHowConsequential.message: Change <visuallyHidden>how consequential the dataset is likely to seem</visuallyHidden>
changeHowConsequential.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Is this dataset likely to be of interest to researchers in its corresponding field of study, to most researchers, or to the general public? How consequential is it likely to seem to that audience or those audiences?”.

readyToBeShared.message: Is this dataset ready to be shared?
readyToBeShared.description: Label for the question about whether the dataset is ready to be shared.

changeReadyToBeShared.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset is ready to be shared</visuallyHidden>
changeReadyToBeShared.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “Is this dataset ready to be shared?”.

anythingMissing.message: What else, if anything, would it be helpful for the researcher to include with this dataset to make it easier to find, understand and reuse in ethical and responsible ways?
anythingMissing.description: Label for the question about whether the dataset is missing anything.

noAnswer.description: Label for when the user has not provided an answer to the question “What else, if anything, would it be helpful for the researcher to include with this dataset to make it easier to find, understand and reuse in ethical and responsible ways?”.

changeAnythingMissing.message: Change <visuallyHidden>if the dataset is missing anything</visuallyHidden>
changeAnythingMissing.description: Link that allows the user to change their answer to the question “What else, if anything, would it be helpful for the researcher to include with this dataset to make it easier to find, understand and reuse in ethical and responsible ways?”.

nowPublish.description: Heading for the section to publish the PREreview after checking their answers.

whatNameWouldYouLikeToUse.message: What name would you like to use?
whatNameWouldYouLikeToUse.description: Label for the question about the name the user would like to use.

selectNameYouWouldLikeToUse.message: Select the name that you would like to use
selectNameYouWouldLikeToUse.description: Error message shown when the user tries to continue without selecting a name to use.

youCanChooseOrcidOrPseudonym.message: You can choose between the name on your ORCID record or your PREreview pseudonym.
youCanChooseOrcidOrPseudonym.description: Information provided about the question “What name would you like to use?”.

whatIsAPrereviewPseudonym.description: Heading for the explanation of what a PREreview pseudonym is.

prereviewPseudonymnExplainer.message: A <definition>PREreview pseudonym</definition> is an alternate name you can use instead of your real name. It is unique and combines a random color and animal. Your pseudonym is ‘{pseudonym}.’
prereviewPseudonymnExplainer.description: Explanation of what a PREreview pseudonym is.

whyUseAPseudonym.message: Using your pseudonym, you can contribute to open dataset review without fearing retribution or judgment that may occur when using your real name. However, using a pseudonym retains an element of accountability.
whyUseAPseudonym.description: Explanation of why one might want to use a pseudonym.

weWillLinkToYourOrcid.message: We’ll link your PREreview to your ORCID iD.
weWillLinkToYourOrcid.description: Text that accompanies the public persona choice for the question “What name would you like to use?”.

weWillOnlyLinkToYourPseudonym.message: We’ll only link your PREreview to others that also use your pseudonym.
weWillOnlyLinkToYourPseudonym.description: Text that accompanies the pseudonym choice for the question “What name would you like to use?”.
